reasoning: Query asks for fireworks (hanabi) festivals but get_festivals returns 312 intangible folk cultural properties — traditional matsuri like Kawagoe Hikawa, none specifically hanabi events. The tool was called without a keyword filter for 花火 so hanabi is entirely absent from results.
missing_data: Actual fireworks festivals (花火大会) such as Sumidagawa, Nagaoka, Tsuchiura. The corpus may lack an event layer for hanabi specifically.
improvement_hint: Add keyword/category filter support to get_festivals (e.g. category=hanabi) or index fireworks events separately so intent-specific retrieval is possible.

reasoning: Japanese query for hanabi (花火大会) but same tool call as L3-01 returns all 312 intangible folk cultural properties without any hanabi events. Language correctly set to ja. Same retrieval gap as L3-01.
missing_data: 花火大会 specific events. Sumidagawa Hanabi, Nagaoka Fireworks, and similar are missing.
improvement_hint: Index fireworks events separately or add semantic/keyword filtering so 花火 queries surface hanabi-specific content rather than all matsuri.

reasoning: Query is about snow festivals in Hokkaido; response shows count=0 local items for Hokkaido but falls back to national_heritage which are unrelated UNESCO ceremonies (Dainichido Bugaku in Akita, Kawagoe Hikawa in Saitama). Sapporo Snow Festival — the most famous answer — is entirely absent.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival, any snow/winter festival data for Hokkaido.
improvement_hint: Add winter/seasonal event data for Hokkaido or ensure Sapporo Snow Festival and similar events appear in the corpus so prefecture-filtered queries can surface them.

reasoning: Chinese query for authentic rural life experience; get_japan_heritage returns cultural narrative stories (Asuka women's history, Kamakura samurai). While Japan Heritage stories can relate to rural areas, the first items shown are purely historical/cultural, not countryside-living experiences.
missing_data: Satoyama experiences, farm stays, rural villages like Shirakawa-go, Iya Valley thatched villages; agri-tourism spots.
improvement_hint: Add semantic search or theme filter (satoyama/nōson/farm-stay) to Japan Heritage and supplement with rural experience venue data so countryside-life queries surface relevant content.

reasoning: Korean query for autumn leaf viewing; search returns 83 results but top entries are places named 紅葉 (Momijidani erosion works, Momiji Hachimangu shrine, Momijiyama Park) — real places but mixed bag. Famous koyo spots like Nikko, Kyoto temples, and Towada are not surfaced.
missing_data: Top-ranked koyo destinations: Nikko Toshogu, Eikan-do Kyoto, Towada-Hachimantai. Seasonal timing information.
improvement_hint: Boost prominence scoring for recognized foliage viewing destinations; add a seasonal/theme attribute so autumn-leaf queries surface experiential spots rather than only name-matched entities.

reasoning: Query seeks quiet non-touristy meditation temples; search_area 修行 returns 79 results including actual temples (Shugyoji, Manjushuin with heritage designations) and mountain 我拝師山 (Shikoku). However noisy DMO portal pages (STAY YAMAGATA) dominate top results rather than genuine ascetic retreat sites.
missing_data: Specific temple zazen/shukubo programs, Koyasan lodgings, mountain training centers like Dewa Sanzan.
improvement_hint: Filter or down-rank boilerplate DMO page snippets; add experience-type tags (shukubo, zazen, ascetic practice) to surface genuine retreat options more prominently.

reasoning: Spanish query asks for northern lights in Japan, which are extremely rare and location-specific (far northern Hokkaido in exceptional solar events). Response returns Sanrio Puroland (amusement park), Oita business tourism site, unrelated DMO chunks — no aurora-watching content at all.
missing_data: Information that aurora viewing is extremely rare in Japan; if any, Hokkaido (Wakkanai, Rishiri) as possible locations during peak solar activity.
improvement_hint: Add a 'low-feasibility' flag or disclaimer for queries about phenomena not reliably observed in Japan (aurora); include rare-event metadata so the agent can accurately set expectations.

reasoning: Query asks to sleep in a 100-year-old house (kominka); get_hotels(Gifu) returns standard hotels/hostels (Airbnb apartment, hostel, Route Inn) without any kominka/traditional property filter. Shirakawa-go area has genuine kominka stays but lodging_type data doesn't distinguish them.
missing_data: Kominka guesthouses in Shirakawa-go, Gokayama, Hida-Furukawa area specifically labeled as traditional/historic.
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type: kominka or property_style attributes to hotel data, and/or integrate with Japan Heritage kominka stay registry so queries about traditional accommodation can be filtered properly.

reasoning: Thai query for off-the-beaten-path cherry blossom spots; 165 results but top items are Sakurada-mon gate, Sakuragi Station, Sakurai Shrine — places named 桜 not necessarily cherry blossom viewing spots. Sakurajima (Kagoshima) does appear. Query intent for 'uncrowded' spots cannot be served from prominence scores.
missing_data: Lesser-known cherry blossom spots with low prominence scores surfaced; ability to inverse-rank by crowd level.
improvement_hint: Add cherry-blossom semantic tag or sakura-viewing category so the query retrieves experiential spots rather than entities whose names happen to contain 桜; add crowd/popularity metadata to support 穴場 filtering.

reasoning: Photography dramatic landscapes query; 52 results include Niigata zekkei (50-man sunflowers, sea cliffs), Ehime winter-to-spring drive spots with named locations, Yamagata (Zao frost trees). Useful DMO editorial content present, but structured attraction-level metadata (coordinates, named landscape) is sparse.
missing_data: Structured wikidata entries for famous scenic landscapes (Aokigahara, Tottori dunes, Takachiho gorge). DMO text is useful but coordinate-less.
improvement_hint: Supplement editorial DMO content with wikidata scenic-spot entries tagged 絶景/景観; extract named landmark coordinates from DMO text for better structured output.

reasoning: Arabic query for outdoor hot springs in nature (not hotels); 72 results include specific outdoor baths: Housenbo Onsen (Ehime, star-sky rotenburo), Haraigawa Onsen (mountain family baths), Sukawa Onsen (1000-person outdoor bath at altitude), Okuchida Garden Hotel. Good geographic variety and descriptive content.
missing_data: Some famous wild rotenburo like Tamagawa Onsen or mixed-gender konyoku options.
improvement_hint: Add rotenburo/outdoor-bath type tag to enable filtering from hotel data; current results are good but could be further enhanced by tagging bath style.

reasoning: Query is about swimming with wild dolphins; results include Kyoto cultural ambassador page, a fish market in Niigata, Notojima Aquarium dolphin show (not wild swimming). No wild dolphin swimming destinations like Mikurajima or Amami appear in 60 results.
missing_data: Mikurajima Island (Tokyo) and Torishima wild dolphin swim spots; Amami Oshima dolphin watching. These are real, well-known spots.
improvement_hint: Add wildlife experience tags (wild-dolphin-swim, whale-watching) to attraction data; DMO scrapes from Mikurajima and Ogasawara should surface these activities.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional architecture untouched by modernization; get_japan_heritage with no filter returns 104 stories starting with Asuka women's history and Kamakura. These are historical stories, not specifically about preserved machizukuri/townscape architecture. Stories about preserved towns (Tsumago, Hagi) exist in the corpus but are buried.
missing_data: Japan Heritage stories specifically about preserved townscapes like Tsumago-Magome, Hagi castle town, Ouchijuku would better match the query.
improvement_hint: Add theme filtering to get_japan_heritage (theme=townscape/architecture) or semantic search so traditional-architecture queries surface the most relevant heritage stories rather than all 104.

reasoning: Vietnamese query for Ainu culture experience in Hokkaido; results are excellent: Upopoy National Ainu Museum (Shiraoi), Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum (Biratori, on Sarugawa River cultural landscape), Kawamura Kaneto Ainu Museum (Asahikawa), Hokkaido Ainu Center, Sapporo Ainu Culture Promotion Center. Comprehensive and accurate.
missing_data: Heritage designation label for Q64576748 needs resolution. Interactive experience programs (craft workshops, dance performances) could enhance.
improvement_hint: Resolve all Wikidata heritage designation QIDs to human-readable labels so agents don't receive raw QID strings like Q64576748 instead of proper designation names.

reasoning: Query for cliff-carved Buddhist statues; top results are exactly right: Usuki Stone Buddhas (National Treasure + Special Historic Site, Oita), Oya Magaibutsu (Special Historic Site, Tochigi), Kiyomizu Magaibutsu (Kagoshima). Heritage designation labels fully resolved and coordinates provided. Excellent retrieval.
missing_data: A few more lesser-known sites could round out coverage, but key sites are all present.
improvement_hint: Minor: Uchikawamagaihi is a cliff inscription not a Buddha statue — could be filtered by adding sub-type classification. Otherwise this is a model response.

reasoning: Day-trip volcano hike from Tokyo query; results return Aso caldera (Kumamoto) content extensively — these are 1000+ km from Tokyo and not day-trippable. Mt. Fuji, Hakone, Izu volcanoes near Tokyo are not surfaced. The distance constraint is completely ignored.
missing_data: Hakone volcanic area (2h from Tokyo), Mt. Fuji Gotemba/Subashiri trails, Izu Oshima volcano — all day-trip feasible from Tokyo.
improvement_hint: Add geographic proximity filtering to search_area or support region/distance constraints so 'from Tokyo' queries surface Kanto/Tokai region results rather than distant Kyushu content.

reasoning: Indonesian query for freshly-made temple tofu experience; results include DISCOVER FUKUI portal, Kumamoto food guide, Iwate ryokan entries, a maff_gi entry for frozen tofu from Iwadeyama. The specific experience of eating temple-made tofu (e.g., Kyoto Nanzenji area, Koyasan shojin ryori) is absent.
missing_data: Temple tofu restaurants near Koyasan, Nanzenji area tofu kaiseki in Kyoto, Nikko tofu dining. Shojin ryori venues with tofu-making experiences.
improvement_hint: Add food-experience tags to attraction/spot data; supplement with restaurant/experience venue data for temple cuisine (shojin ryori, tofu-making workshops) so culinary-experience queries can be served.

reasoning: Query for fishing villages frozen in time; 81 results include Kesennuma harbor town description (excellent, with rias coastline, traditional fishing culture), fish market in Niigata (Teradomari). Kesennuma DMO text is highly relevant. However purely traditional 'frozen in time' villages like Ine no Funaya boat houses are not prominently surfaced.
missing_data: Ine no Funaya (Kyoto), Tomo no Ura (Hiroshima) preserved fishing harbor, Nishiizu Dogashima area.
improvement_hint: Add heritage-fishing-village tag; Ine no Funaya is an Important Cultural Landscape — ensure cultural landscape designations for fishing settlements appear in search results.

reasoning: Washi papermaking query; get_local_specialty craft returns 231 METI traditional craft items starting with Nibutani Attus textile and Oitama Tsumugi. Washi paper items exist in this dataset but are not in the first visible items. The tool returns good structured data but ranking doesn't prioritize washi/paper for this query.
missing_data: Echizen Washi, Mino Washi (Gifu), Kurotani Washi near Kyoto — specific papermaking villages with workshop experiences.
improvement_hint: Add semantic/keyword search parameter to get_local_specialty so q='和紙' or category='paper' surfaces washi items at top; current alphabetic/index ordering buries the most relevant crafts.

reasoning: Japanese query for period-drama-like landscapes; 宿場 search surfaces Nakasendo post towns Suharashuku and Nojiroshuku (Nagano), Yanagimachi district in Ueda — highly relevant, well-described with historical architecture. Also Tokaido cycling routes. Good practical content with names, URLs, descriptions.
missing_data: Tsumago-Magome (most iconic Nakasendo post towns) not visible in top results. Kyoto backstreets or Kanazawa higashi chaya district could also appear.
improvement_hint: Minor: Akasaka Detached Palace (迎賓館) appears mid-results — it is a Western-style building, not period-drama scenery. Better ranking would suppress it for this query.

reasoning: Query for uncrowded beaches; results return actual beaches (Naminoue Beach Okinawa, Hamasaka Beach Hyogo, Sunayama Beach Miyako, Yonehara Beach Ishigaki, Tropical Beach Okinawa). However these are well-known spots — not 'uncrowded' alternatives. The corpus has no crowd-level metadata to distinguish hidden vs. popular beaches.
missing_data: Crowd/popularity metadata; lesser-known beaches like Shirahama in Wakayama non-peak, Iriomote beaches, Shimokita Peninsula remote beaches.
improvement_hint: Add popularity/crowding tier metadata to beach spots; enable relevance re-ranking that down-weights high-tourism spots when 穴場 or 'not crowded' intent is detected.

reasoning: Query for old-school izakaya alleys to drink with locals; 横丁 search returns a park in Shinagawa, a Saga tourism overview, unrelated Yamaguchi attraction, Sumo monument. Actual izakaya-alley spots like Omoide Yokocho (Tokyo), Dotonbori alleys, or Nishiki Market area are entirely absent.
missing_data: Omoide Yokocho (Shinjuku), Nonbei Yokocho (Shibuya), Haruyoshi alley (Fukuoka), Tanuki Koji (Sapporo) — real izakaya alleys.
improvement_hint: Add nightlife/dining-alley category to spot data; tag known yokocho/izakaya alleys explicitly so social-drinking intent queries can retrieve them. Current 横丁 term-match retrieves too broadly.

reasoning: German query for rural train travel; 57 results include high-quality content: Nagano Ueda Dentetsu Bessho Line description with schedule/fare info, Ibaraki Kashima Rinkai Railway + Hitachi Seaside Park itinerary, Tsugaru DMO mentioning 2 local railways. Real named lines with practical details. Good multilingual coverage.
missing_data: Iconic routes like SL Banetsu Monogatari, Iya Valley trains, or Oigawa Railway steam train. German translation of key content absent.
improvement_hint: Add train-type/scenic-railway tags to transport data; currently relies on DMO editorial which is good but irregular. A dedicated scenic-railway dataset would improve consistency.

reasoning: Firefly viewing query; 73 results include Toyoda Firefly Museum (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi), Hotaru-no-Sato park (Hyogo), Yamaguchi hotaru boat-ride on Kiya River (national monument genji-firefly habitat, dates: June 5-21, price ¥2000/adult), Kanazawa Kenrokuen firefly event. Named sites with dates and practical details.
missing_data: Genji firefly spots in Tochigi (Utsunomiya area) or Shizuoka Shimanto River areas. Seasonal timing for each region.
improvement_hint: Minor: the Yamaguchi spot description has excellent event-level detail (dates, prices) — this pattern should be more consistently extracted and structured rather than left as raw text.

reasoning: Russian query for winter crane watching; 165 results for 鶴 but top hits are Tsurugaoka Hachimangu shrine (Kamakura), Kakurinji temple buildings, Tsuruma Park (Nagoya) — places with 鶴 in name. Izumi city in Kagoshima (world's largest crane wintering site) is absent from visible results.
missing_data: Izumi Crane Observatory (Kagoshima), Akan Crane Center (Hokkaido), Kushiro wetlands crane feeding — all real, well-known winter crane watching spots.
improvement_hint: Add wildlife/bird-watching semantic tags; 鶴 (crane) as a wildlife query should be distinguished from 鶴 as a place-name character. A bird-watching or wildlife category would let these queries surface the correct content.
